stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (announced April 21, 2026): revenue Rs 4,055.9 cr (+25% YoY); USD revenue $436.0M (+3.2% QoQ); PAT Rs 529.2 cr (+33.7% YoY, +20% QoQ); EBIT margin expanded to 16.3% from 14.4% QoQ (+190 bps). FY26 revenue $1,654.4M (+17.4% YoY); EBIT margin 15.6% with EBIT growth +31.1% YoY. Q4 TCV bookings $600.8M; ACV $445.1M; TTM new TCV $1,465.8M. Final dividend Rs 18/share; FY26 total dividend Rs 40/share. 24th consecutive quarter of growth. Stock at Rs 4,816.30 May 5; fell ~5% post-results on margin miss vs Street expectations and growth slowing perception. CLSA Outperform TP Rs 8,865; Nomura Neutral TP Rs 6,100; MarketsMojo Hold (downgraded from Buy Feb 5). Trading near 52w low Rs 4,449.10 (52w high Rs 6,599).
stocks.sector_context: Indian IT sector under pressure - Nifty IT down 0.77% to 29,043.50 on May 7. Mid-cap IT/ER&D facing AI disruption fears, US discretionary spend uncertainty. However, weak rupee (95+) supports export earnings, providing tailwind for IT exporters. Mid-cap IT lags but seen as positioned for outperformance if Nifty stays above 24,000. Persistent considered a high-quality AI/digital engineering play within the cluster.
stocks.peer_comparison: Q4 revenue growth +25% YoY ahead of large-cap IT peers (TCS/Infosys mid-single-digit). EBIT margin expansion +190 bps QoQ best-in-class. Premium P/E ~40x reflects growth quality. Among mid-cap IT, Persistent's TCV/ACV consistency superior to Coforge/LTIMindtree on visibility. Stock down sharper than sector since results - relative weakness in near term despite operational outperformance.
red_flags: Stock fell ~5% post-Q4 results on margin miss vs Street expectations
Trading near 52w low Rs 4,449 (CMP ~Rs 4,816) suggests technical weakness persists
MarketsMojo downgraded Buy to Hold on Feb 5, 2026
Mid-cap IT sector under structural pressure from AI disruption narrative
Margin expansion strong QoQ but FY26 EBIT margin 15.6% still below Tier-1 levels
Dispersed brokerage views (CLSA TP Rs 8,865 vs Nomura Rs 6,100) signal disagreement on trajectory
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 18/share (FY26 total Rs 40/share) - record date forthcoming
Q4 TCV $600.8M signals strong FY27 revenue conversion runway
EBIT margin expansion trajectory (16.3% Q4 vs 14.4% Q3)
Weak rupee (95+) supports export earnings tailwind
CLSA Outperform TP Rs 8,865 (~84% upside) on AI/digital engineering thesis
24th sequential growth quarter establishes execution credibility

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (announced May 4, 2026): consolidated PAT Rs 1,129.16 cr (+30.6% YoY); revenue Rs 15,877 cr (+12% YoY). FY26 PAT Rs 3,797 cr (+21%); revenue Rs 53,871 cr (+14%). Lending portfolio (NBFC + HFC) Rs 2,07,368 cr (+32% YoY, +9% QoQ); NBFC AUM Rs 1,59,916 cr (+27%); HFC AUM Rs 47,452 cr (+53%). Total AUM (AMC + life + health) Rs 5,91,343 cr (+16%). Health insurance GWP +39%; life FYP +15%. Borrowing limit raised to Rs 2 lakh cr. All-time high Rs 372.5 on May 6, 2026; trading Rs 363.30 on May 8 (mcap Rs 96,697 cr). Three-session winning streak (+7.66%) into May 6. Macquarie reaffirmed Outperform TP Rs 415 (~18% upside); Morgan Stanley Overweight; some brokerages flag up to +24% upside.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Financial Services constructive - benefiting from positive credit-cycle sentiment, government scheme rollouts (ECLGS), and steady AUM/insurance premium growth. Diversified financial conglomerates rerating vs pure-play NBFCs as cross-sell within ecosystems gains traction. Insurance gross premiums and AMC AUM expansion supportive across the cluster.
stocks.peer_comparison: ABCAPITAL +93.94% over 1 year - among the strongest large NBFC-conglomerate runs. Q4 PAT +30.6% ahead of typical large-cap NBFC pace. 5Y operating profit CAGR +26.03% best-in-class. Diversified mix (NBFC + HFC + AMC + life + health insurance) offers earnings stability vs pure-play NBFCs. HFC AUM growth +53% YoY notable vs sector book ~+14%.
red_flags: Stock at fresh all-time high - momentum extended after +94% 1Y rally
Margin pressure flagged in some sell-side commentary despite PAT beat
NBFC sector valuation re-rating may be near peak; risk of mean reversion
Sequential lending growth +9% QoQ vs +32% YoY shows some moderation
catalysts: Macquarie Outperform TP Rs 415 (~18% upside) post-Q4
Continued benefit from ECLGS / government credit scheme rollout
AUM and lending book scaling momentum into FY27
Health insurance GWP +39% trajectory if sustained
Borrowing limit raise to Rs 2 lakh cr unlocks growth headroom

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 board meeting confirmed May 11, 2026 (results + possible interim dividend for FY27); earnings call May 12 at 1 PM IST with CEO Ashish Kehair and CFO Bharat Kalsi. Plan to invest Rs 100 cr in subsidiary NWFL's rights issue. Q3 FY26 (Jan): Wealth client assets Rs 1,11,356 cr (+9% YoY) with MPIS +30%; Private client assets Rs 2,17,691 cr (+4%) with ARR +24%; Q3 revenue Rs 755 cr (+4% YoY). Stock at Rs 1,561.80 on May 8 (6M +6.97%, 1Y +30.98%). NSE penalty Rs 8,69,600 on subsidiary NWIL April 22, 2026 (margin statement reporting); two NSE warning letters April 29, 2026 on NWIL.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Financial Services constructive. Wealth management peers riding broader market participation, AUM growth, and HNI/UHNI demand. Capital markets activity remains supportive but wealth-tech segment seeing episodic regulatory scrutiny across the cluster.
stocks.peer_comparison: Nuvama 1-yr +30.98%, 6-mo +6.97% - underperforms diversified financials peer ABCAPITAL (+93.94% 1Y). Q3 revenue growth (+4% YoY) trails sector AUM-growth pace. Asset growth concentrated in MPIS (+30%) and ARR (+24%) - quality mix improving. Pure-play wealth manager vs diversified conglomerate competitors.
red_flags: NSE penalty Rs 8,69,600 on subsidiary NWIL on April 22, 2026 (margin statement reporting)
Two NSE warning letters to NWIL on April 29, 2026 (periodical submission observations)
Multiple regulatory pings on subsidiary in 3-week span - pattern risk even if individually small
Q3 revenue growth of only +4% YoY - sub-sector pace
Pre-results headline risk into May 11 board meeting (T-3 days)
Promoter mix (PAG + Edelweiss legacy) - any future stake action overhang
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results on May 11, 2026 (board meeting)
Possible interim FY27 dividend declaration on May 11
Earnings call May 12 - regulatory commentary and FY27 outlook
Rs 100 cr capital infusion into NWFL strengthening subsidiary base
MPIS (+30%) and ARR (+24%) asset mix momentum

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (announced May 4-5, 2026): revenue Rs 1,572.22 cr (+22% YoY, +15% QoQ); operating EBITDA Rs 252.1 cr (margin 16.0% vs 14.1% QoQ but down from 18.2% YoY); PAT Rs 204.17 cr (+8% YoY). FY26 revenue Rs 5,505.57 cr (vs Rs 5,168.45 cr); FY26 PAT Rs 546.59 cr (DOWN from Rs 676.95 cr in FY25 = -19%). Final dividend Rs 8.35/share + special dividend Rs 3.35/share = Rs 11.70 total. Stock surged 9.11% on May 5 to Rs 655 intraday on dividend cheer; last trade Rs 644.90. Es-Tec GmbH (Germany) acquisition completed Nov 27, 2025. MOFSL Sell rating with TP Rs 500 (~22% downside).
stocks.sector_context: Indian IT services under structural pressure in 2026 - Nifty IT down materially YTD on AI disruption fears, US discretionary spend uncertainty. Nifty IT at 29,043 (-0.77% May 7). ER&D segment slightly more resilient but auto vertical recovery still early-stage. Mid-cap IT/ER&D earnings dispersion wide; AI-led engineering and SDV (Software-Defined Vehicle) transitions favoured by sell-side.
stocks.peer_comparison: Q4 revenue growth (+22% YoY) ahead of large-cap IT peers. ER&D peer P/E: KPIT ~25.8x, LTTS ~32.8x; TataTech forward P/E ~29x is premium relative to growth/peers per MOFSL. Choice prefers KPIT for SDV-led work; LTTS favoured for AI/data-centre engineering. ROE compressed to 14.6% in FY26 (vs 19.8/21.9/23.7% in prior years) - profitability lags peers despite revenue beat.
red_flags: FY26 PAT DECLINED to Rs 547 cr from Rs 677 cr (-19%) despite revenue growth
EBITDA margin compressed YoY (16.0% vs 18.2%) - structural margin pressure
ROE collapsed to 14.6% in FY26 (vs 19.8-23.7% historical)
MOFSL Sell with TP Rs 500 (~22% downside vs CMP)
Premium valuation (~29x fwd P/E) vs ER&D peers KPIT (~25.8x) and growth profile
Auto ER&D recovery early-stage; growth depends on execution of recent wins
Stock +9% on dividend pop near term overextends entry
catalysts: Final + special dividend Rs 11.70/share (record date forthcoming)
Es-Tec GmbH integration synergies into FY27
1QFY27 expected revenue/EBIT growth +28.6%/+32.7% YoY (sell-side estimate)
Auto ER&D demand recovery if it materialises in FY27
Margin recovery (16.0% in Q4 vs 14.1% prior quarter)

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 914.55 on May 5, 2026; trading 24.7% below 52w high of Rs 1,214.5 (52w low Rs 860.1). Market cap Rs 21,610 cr. Q4 FY26 results not yet released (cement majors typically report mid-May). Q3 FY26 (most recent): revenue Rs 2,105.68 cr; PAT Rs 385.11-385.59 cr (+111.42% YoY - low base effect). Q2 FY26 PAT Rs 77.34 cr (+200.93% YoY) on revenue +9.52% YoY. Capacity expansion to 30 MTPA underway with Rs 1,403 cr capex. Sell-side mixed: Citi TP Rs 1,140, Ambit Rs 950 - both above CMP. Early monsoon onset (May 24 last year context) historically pressures South cement demand into Q1.
stocks.sector_context: Indian cement sector: demand strong (7-8% CAGR FY24-27 expected) driven by housing/infrastructure, but industry-wide capacity expansion of 158 mn tonnes through FY28 (7.4% CAGR) raising oversupply concerns. South India remains the weakest pricing region; dealers offering discounts ahead of seasonal dip. UltraTech (~50x P/E) and Shree Cement (~53x P/E) lead the cluster on multiples. Operating profit/tonne expected at Rs 900-950, +12-18% YoY, on stable input costs.
stocks.peer_comparison: Heavy South India exposure where pricing remains soft - underperforms pan-India peers (UltraTech, ACC, Ambuja) on realisation. Ongoing capex pressures balance sheet; smaller scale than top-3. Stock down 24.7% from 52w high while UltraTech/Shree have held up better. Ramco and Dalmia Bharat noted as down ~1% on early monsoon/southern price pressure.
red_flags: Heavy South India exposure where cement prices remain weak
Capex-heavy expansion (Rs 1,403 cr ongoing) pressuring leverage during pricing weakness
Industry oversupply risk from 158 mn tonnes incoming capacity through FY28
Stock down 24.7% from 52w high despite sector resilience elsewhere
Q4 FY26 results pending - pre-event uncertainty (mid-May expected)
Q3 PAT +111% growth was on a low base; underlying realisations remain weak
Pre-monsoon seasonal demand softness in South region
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results and FY27 outlook (mid-May expected)
South India cement price recovery if realised
Monsoon-driven demand normalisation in H2 FY27
Final dividend declaration on results
30 MTPA capacity ramp delivers operating leverage if realisations recover
Citi TP Rs 1,140 (~25% upside) and Ambit Rs 950 (~4%) above CMP
